texts.full_text: moth of the family Gracillariidae. It is known from Hong Kong, Japan (the Ryukyu Islands), India (West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Bihar, Delhi and Uttar Pradesh) and Réunion. It has recently been recorded from Europe. The habitat consists of secondary forests and cultivated areas. The wingspan is 6-7 mm. The forewings are grey with a narrow, pale, oblique fascia before the middle and a broader, pale, oblique fascia beyond the middle, both with some dark markings and with the dorsal edge dark. There is a dark brown tornal spot and a narrow, pale, terminal fascia, both with some dark markings. The hindwings are pale grey with a long, pale, slender, whitish-yellowish erect basal hair-pencil. Adults are on wing in March, May, June, August and October. The larvae feed on Adenia species (including Adenia heterophylla), Cocculus species (including Cocculus hirsutus), Cissus species (including Cissus repanda and Cissus rhombifolia), Vitis species (including Vitis vinifera) and Leea species (including Leea

texts.full_text: n About UsJazz CalendarHall of FameNewslettersPhoto GalleryJazz LinksDonationContact UsAnnouncements About the Des Moines Community Jazz Center Hall of Fame Des Moines may not be the JAZZ "capital of the world", but that hasn't stopped the Greater Des Moines Community Jazz Center (CJC) from recognizing the musical contribuitions of the city's finest and most influential musicians. Started in 2001, the Des Moines Jazz Hall of Fame was established to hoor those musicians who have been proud to call Des Moines (or Central Iowa) home. Musicians selected for the Des moines Jazz Hall of Fame have not only influenced future generations of musicians, but have provided the city's jazz fans with years of musical adventure and enjoyment. Each October since 2001, DJazz enthusiasts gather to celebrate at our annual induction ceremony where new members are welcomed into this prestigious group. The event serves as a reminder of Des Moines' rich history in Jazz music and honors the legacy that these musicians have left behind. The Hall of Fame is more than just an accolade; it's a testament to the city’s commitment to nurturing talent, fostering creativity, and preserving its cultural heritage. The induction process involves rigorous selection based on various criteria such as musical innovation, impactful contributions to jazz education or community development within Des Moines. Over two decades later, our Hall of Fame continues to grow with each passing year, reflecting the vibrant and ever-evolving landscape of Jazz in this region. It stands not only as a tribute but also an inspiration for aspiring musicians who dream of making their mark on the world through music. Whether you're a seasoned jazz aficionado or simply appreciate good tunes, exploring our Hall of Fame is like taking a journey back to Des Moines' musical roots while discovering its future sounds. texts.full_text: Is Saturated Fat Bad for You? A new review suggests that saturated fats, like those found in may dairy products and meat, may not be the big contributors to heart disease or early death that many think they are. However, the Canadian researchers who did the review did find a clear link between heart troubles and trans fats, which are found in highly processed foods such as snacks, margarine and baked goods. “Not all the studies we looked at reached the same conclusion, but generally what we found is that the association between a higher consumption of trans fats and a higher risk for heart disease and [early] death was very consistent,” said study author Russell de Souza. “And because we found no evidence that trans fat offers any health benefit, removing it from the foods we eat is the right idea,” added de Souza, a registered dietician and an assistant professor of clinical epidemiology & biostatistics at McMaster University in Hamilton, Ontario. “On the other hand, the association between consuming saturated fat and a higher risk for similar health issues was variable and unclear,” he added. “But we want to be careful. We’re not saying that we’re confident that saturated fat is truly benign.” De Souza and his colleagues reported their findings in the August 11 issues of the BMJBased on the new review, it seems that saturated fats may not be as detrimental to heart health as previously thought, but trans fats found in highly processed foods still pose a significant risk for heart disease and early death. The researchers emphasize the importance of removing trans fat from our diets due to its lack of any known benefits. While more research is needed to fully understand the effects of saturated fat on health, it appears that focusing on reducing trans fat consumption may be beneficial in promoting better heart health

texts.full_text: ircuit Judges. PER CURIAM. 1 Douglas John Knight, a federal inmate, filed a 28 U.S.C. Sec. 2255 motion, arguing the Bureau of Prisons (BOP) erroneously refused to credit his sentence with time he spent released on bail subject to very restrictive conditions. The district court1 denied Knight's motion. We affirm. 2 Regardless of the conditions to which Knight was subjected, he was not detained at a penal or correctional facility, and thus cannot receive credit toward his sentence for the time he spent released on bail. See Reno v. Koray, 115 S. Ct. 2021, 2025-26 (1995) (credit towards sentence under Sec. 3585(b) available only for those defendants detained in penal or correctional facility and under control of BOP). 3 We reject Knight's argument that applying Koray, which was decided during the pendency of his appeal, violates his due process rights. At the time Knight chose to accept restrictive bail, he was not entitled to receive credit toward his sentence in either the Second Circuit, where he was arrested, or the Eighth Circuit. Renewable energy does not include energy resources derived from fossil fuels, waste products from fossil sources, or waste products from inorganic sources." - Texas Renewable Energy Industries Alliance Hydropower (or hydroelectric power) refers to the use of energy from naturally falling or flowing water to generate electricity. The kinetic energy from the falling water is converted to mechanical energy and then to electricity. Water constantly goes through a vast global cycle. It evaporates from lakes, rrivers, oceans, even your morning cup of coffee due to heat provided by sunlight or other sources like geothermal activity beneath Earth's surface. This water vapor eventually condenses and falls back down as precipitation (rain, snow), starting the cycle anew. Hydropower harnesses this natural process using dams that store potential energy in reservoirs until it is needed for electricity generation or flood control purposes. When released through turbines at controlled rates based on demand levels set by grid operators and utility companies alike - voila! You have clean, reliable power ready to be distributed across homes & businesses nearby without burning any fossil fuels whatsoever nor emitting harmful greenhouse gases into our atmosphere which contributes significantly towards climate change mitigation efforts worldwide. texts.full_text: anNPCs (Non-Player Characters) in role-playing games like Dungeons & Dragons serve as entities that players interact with during their adventures. The definition provided from Third Edition Player's Handbook clarifies several aspects: 1. Control: NPCs are controlled by the Dungeon Master, not a player. 2. Character vs Creature: - An NPC can be any character within the game setting that is under DM control. - While "character" often implies some level of personality or role (like townsfolk), it's frequently used interchangeably with “creature,” which refers to all living beings in a fantasy world. 3. Intelligence and Class Levels: - Intelligence isn't necessarily required for an NPC; many creatures like oozes, animals such as dogs, plants, constructs including golems can be NPCs if they are controlled by the DM. - An intelligence or class level is not mandatory either. A creature could simply serve a purpose in advancing plot points without needing these attributes. 4. Examples: - Ooze: Yes, it's an NPC because even though its actions might seem instinctual rather than intelligent (controlled mechanically), if the DM decides to use one as part of their storytelling or world-building during gameplay. - Dog: Similarly a dog can be considered an NPC when controlled by the Dungeon Master for specific interactions within that session. 5. Purpose: NPCs serve various roles such as providing information, posing challenges (like monsters), acting out plot elements, and enriching game narratives through interaction with player characters. In summary, any creature or character in a D&D setting can be an NPC if it is controlled by the DM for narrative purposes during gameplay. This includes creatures

texts.full_text: ison of two warfarin regimens in the prevention of venous thrombosis following total knee replacement. A prospective, randomized trial was conducted to compare the effectiveness and safety of warfarin given in two regimens in prevention of venous thrombosis after total knee replacement. Adult patients scheduled for primary or revision total knee replacement were randomly assigned to receive either a "two-step" warfarin regimen beginning 10-14 days pre-operatively or, alternatively, to begin warfarin the night before surgery. Post-operatively, the dose was adjusted in both groups to achieve a target International Normalized Ratio (INR) of 2.2 and prophylaxis was continued until venography on post-operative days five through nine. texts.full_text: f the literature to assess predictive equations of REE in critically ill children. We systematically searched the literature for studies, and then we extracted data and assigned a quality grade to each article according to guidelines of the academy of Nutrition and Dietetics. Accuracy was defined as the percentage of predicted REE values to fall within ±10% or ±15% of the measured energy expenditure (MEE) values, computed based on individual participant data. Of the 993 identified studies, 22 studies testing 21 equations using 2326 IC measurements in 1102 children were included in this review. Only 6 equations were evaluated by at least 3 studies in critically ill children. No equation predicted REE within ±10% of MEE in >50% of observations. The Harris-Benedict equation overestimated REE in two-thirds of patients, whereas the Schofield equations and Talbot tables predicted REE within ±15% of MEE in approximately 50% of observations. texts.full_text: In N.M. for PTSD Vanessa Molina, 30, a U.S. Army Reserve staff sergeant, survived a roadside bomb attack in 2006 during her second of three tours in Iraq. She says treatment at the New Mexico VA helps her contend with symptoms of traumatic brain injury and PTSD. (marla brose/journal) FOR THE RECORD – This story incorrectly reported that New Mexico lacks inpatient treatment for post-traumatic stress disorder. New Mexico lacks inpatient treatment for patients with both traumatic brain injury and PTSD, but the Albuquerque VA Medical Center does have a 24-bed residential treatment program for veterans who are dealing with both substance use and PTSD. The New Mexico VA Health Care System has no inpatient beds for veterans with post traumatic stress syndrome, which requires some New Mexico veterans to seek care out of stThe article discusses the availability of inpatient care for PTSD in New Mexico, highlighting that the New Mexico VA Health Care System lacks inpatient beds specifically for PTSD. However, the Albuquerque VA Medical Center offers a 24-bed residential program for veterans dealing with both PTSD and substance use. This means that while there are resources for those with co-occurring conditions, veterans with PTSD alone must seek care out-of-state. The situation underscores the challenges in mental health care accessibility for veterans and the need for expanded resources. It's a complex issue that may extend beyond New Mexico, reflecting broader challenges in the VA system. The correction in the article is crucial, clarifying the limited inpatient options and the specialized programs available.

texts.full_text: e microtubule network in fission yeast. The localization of microtubules to the cell end is essential for proper cell division and has been shown to be regulated by the interplay between these two proteins. The discovery that mal3p and tip1p cooperate to target microtubules to cell ends suggests that their actions are coordinated and that their roles are not mutually exclusive. Further research is needed to elucidate the molecular mechanisms by which mal3p and tip1p coordinate their actions and to determine the precise role of mal3p in the organization of the microtubule cytoskeleton. texts.full_text: ique. In the method described, the head of a fixed-stage microscope is equipped with an inverted objective lens. A glass capillary tube is then filled with cell suspension and placed near the stage's surface under the control of fine manipulators. The process begins by focusing on one specific area where cells are to be impaled, using low magnification (around x10). The operator adjusts both coarse and fine focus knobs until a clear image appears. Once in position, switch over to high power objective lens (usually around 40x or higher) for precise operation. Under the microscope's field of view, gently approach one cell with your capillary tube using manipulators at low speed so as not to disturb other cells and surroundings. The key is a slow but steady hand movement that allows you to precisely position yourself over the target without causing any damage by too much force or sudden movements. When close enough (usually about 1-2mm above), stop moving, hold your breath if necessary for stability of hands, then with one quick yet controlled motion push
